candidate_summary: K-draft phase-offset interlocking speculative decoder. K small drafter models D_1..D_K each emit ONE token at output positions p where p mod K = i. Single target model verifies K-token batch cohesively in one forward pass. On partial rejection, accepted prefix kept, rejected positions re-drafted.

results.name: MetaSD Multi-Drafter Speculative 2604.05417
results.description: multi-drafter framework with multi-armed-bandit drafter selection
results.rationale: Has multiple drafters but uses multi-armed-bandit selection rather than phase-offset interleaving. Drafters do not each emit at position p mod K = i; instead one drafter is selected per step. No hocket-style interlocking. Semantic similarity exists in 'multi-drafter speculative' concept but functional mechanism differs substantially.

results.name: ParallelSpec OpenReview SXvb8PS4Ud
results.description: parallel drafter scheme for efficient speculative decoding
results.rationale: Parallel drafter scheme conceptually adjacent but does not specify K independent drafters keyed to position-modulo phases. No interlocking single-note alternation. Generic parallel drafting without phase-offset position assignment.

results.name: P-EAGLE Parallel Speculative vLLM AWS 2026
results.rationale: Uses MASK placeholders with position incrementing in a single drafter, not K independent drafters at phase-offset positions. Mechanism is mask-based parallel speculation, not multi-drafter interlocking. No phase modulo-K assignment to distinct drafter models.

results.name: Parallel Token Prediction ICLR 2026 2512.21323
results.rationale: Interleaved-mask scheme has surface similarity to 'interleaved' in candidate, but uses masks within a single model rather than K distinct drafter models assigned to phase-offset positions. Quadratic decoding is unrelated to single-pass batch verification of K-token cohesive output.

results.name: Multi-Token Prediction 2507.11851
results.rationale: Generic parallel candidate generation. No K-draft phase-offset structure, no position-modulo-K assignment, no interlocking alternation. Different functional mechanism.

results.name: DART Diffusion-Inspired Speculative 2601.19278
results.rationale: Diffusion-inspired denoising approach is mechanistically unrelated to K-drafter phase-offset interlocking. No interleaved drafter assignment, no hocket-style alternation.

results.name: Google TPU Diffusion-Style Speculative 3x speedup
results.rationale: Diffusion-style mechanism, not multi-drafter phase-offset. No K-draft position modulo K assignment. Different functional family.

results.name: Speculative Decoding Survey NAACL 2025
results.rationale: Survey paper covers speculative decoding broadly but no specific match to K-draft phase-offset interlocking hocket mechanism. Generic coverage does not constitute prior art for the specific phase-coherence form.

decision_rationale: No web result reaches keyword_overlap_count >= 2, semantic_score >= 0.7, or functional_score >= 0.7. The candidate's distinguishing mechanism (K independent drafter models, each emitting ONE token at positions p mod K = i, verified cohesively in one target pass) is not anticipated by: (1) bandit-based drafter selection (MetaSD), (2) generic parallel drafter pools (ParallelSpec), (3) mask-placeholder single-drafter parallel schemes (P-EAGLE, ParallelTokenPrediction), (4) multi-token prediction heads (2507.11851), or (5) diffusion-style approaches (DART, Google TPU). The phase-offset modulo-K assignment of distinct drafters to interleaved positions is the novel hocket-coherence signature and does not appear in any of the 8 results.
